user_message: How should I coach Raj Mehta on Azure Ridge Partners? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 13:27 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 08:25 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Thomas Okafor <thomas.okafor@azureridgepa.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 13:12 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Azure Ridge Partners
Date: 2024-01-01
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), David (Azure Ridge Partners)

[00:02:00] Thomas Okafor (Azure Ridge Partners): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:09:00] David (Azure Ridge Partners): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] David (Azure Ridge Partners): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:19:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:22:00] David (Azure Ridge Partners): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:25:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-F trap (Omits likely objections section); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-F failure mode (Omits likely objections section)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
